channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Israel and Lebanon prepare for talks amid escalated violence affecting Iran truce
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced plans to engage in direct talks with Lebanon following a devastating series of bombings that killed over 300 people, complicating the US-brokered ce...

item.title: Sri Lankan authorities detain Indian fishermen for fishing in restricted waters
item.description: The Sri Lankan Navy has apprehended ten Indian fishermen for allegedly engaging in poaching within Sri Lankan territorial waters. The incident reportedly occurred near the Palk Strait, a region known ...

item.title: Analyzing the Cultural Impact and Enduring Fame of Kanye West
item.description: The article explores the reasons behind Kanye West's enduring fame, highlighting his impact on music, fashion, and popular culture. Kanye's influence extends beyond his albums, as he has shaped trends...

item.title: Labor’s proposed gambling ad reforms explained and how they differ from Peta Murphy’s report
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese recently revealed the government’s long-anticipated plans to tackle gambling advertising in Australia, announcing that the new framework is scheduled to take effect on ...

item.title: CAF President Denies Corruption Allegations After Senegal’s AFCON Title Reversal
item.description: The president of the Confederation of African Football (CAF), Patrice Motsepe, has strongly denied accusations of corruption made by the Senegalese government after CAF’s controversial decision to str...

item.title: Why Kenyans Are Losing Faith in President Ruto’s Leadership
item.description: In his opinion piece, Mutuma Mathiu paints a detailed and rather somber picture of the growing frustration among Kenyans with President William Ruto’s administration. Citing recent surveys, he notes t...

item.title: Nigerian Navy Transfers Two Stowaway Suspects to Immigration for Investigation
item.description: The Nigerian Navy Ship (NNS) BEECROFT don hand over two men wey dem suspect say be stowaways to Nigeria Immigration Service (NIS) for further check. The suspects, Abdullahi Yakubu, 36, and Omoniyi Afo...

item.title: Western Cape Provides R22 Million to Support Farmers Facing Drought
item.description: The Western Cape Department of Agriculture has stepped in to assist livestock farmers struggling with the impacts of prolonged dry conditions. An allocation of R22 million has been approved as risk re...

item.title: Hunter Biden Issues Challenge to Trump’s Sons for a Potential Cage Match
item.description: In a surprising turn of events, Hunter Biden, son of former US President Joe Biden, has publicly challenged Donald Trump’s eldest sons, Donald Jr. and Eric, to a cage match. The challenge reportedly c...

item.title: TMPD director admits role in Tshwane’s wasteful expenditure during Madlanga commission hearing
item.description: Tshwane Metro Police Department (TMPD) director for asset protection services, Tshukudu Malatji, has acknowledged his responsibility for wasteful expenditure within the city. Testifying before the Mad...

item.title: Grab suspends driver after lewd comments to actress Xyriel Manabat
item.description: A Grab driver has been suspended following allegations of making lewd remarks towards actress Xyriel Manabat. The driver posted suggestive comments alongside a photo he took with the actress, which sp...

item.title: Current Situation in the Middle East as Tensions Escalate Between Israel, Iran, and the US
item.description: The latest news coverage from GMA Network on April 10, 2026, reports continuing violence and military activity in the Middle East, particularly involving Israel, Lebanon, Iran, and the United States. ...

item.title: Nigeria to Host 2026 Commonwealth Fencing Championships in Lagos
item.description: Nigeria don make history as e go be di first sub-Saharan country wey go host di Commonwealth Fencing Championships for 2026. Di tournament go hold for Lagos from August 9 to 14, and di venue wey dem c...

item.title: UN nuclear chief voices concern over recent attack near Iran’s Bushehr nuclear plant
item.description: The head of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA), Rafael Grossi, expressed deep concern after reports of a projectile attack near Iran's Bushehr nuclear power plant on April 4, 2026. This mar...

item.title: B.C. forestry conference highlights DRIPA and industry sustainability concerns
item.description: The annual forestry conference in British Columbia has convened more than 600 representatives from industry, government, and First Nations to discuss ongoing challenges in the province's forestry sect...

item.title: Kenya Lags Behind: Amenya Highlights Development Disparities with Addis Ababa and Kigali
item.description: A scathing commentary by Nelson Amenya highlights the growing frustration amongst Kenyans regarding the country's developmental progress compared to neighboring nations like Ethiopia and Rwanda. Ameny...

item.title: Manitoba seeks military support as Peguis First Nation braces for severe spring flooding
item.description: The Government of Manitoba has requested assistance from the Canadian Armed Forces to help Peguis First Nation prepare for an anticipated spring flood that could cause severe damage. Premier Wab Kinew...

item.title: Han Kang's 'Light and Thread': A Reflection on Korean Literature and Cultural Echoes
item.description: This article explores Han Kang's collection 'Light and Thread,' a work that delves into the nuances of Korean literature, cultural values, and the deliberate use of silence. The piece reflects on how ...

item.title: Russia declares 32-hour ceasefire over Orthodox Easter in Ukraine
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin has announced a 32-hour unilateral ceasefire in Ukraine to coincide with the Orthodox Easter weekend. The ceasefire is set to begin at 4pm on Saturday and last until t...
